title: Koyfin Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Koyfin pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Koyfin Pricing Overview

Koyfin is a Bloomberg-style financial data terminal designed for retail investors and professional advisors who need institutional-grade data without the $24,000+ annual Bloomberg Terminal price tag. The platform offers four pricing tiers, starting with a completely free option and scaling up to $119/month for power users. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in New York, Koyfin has positioned itself as an affordable alternative for fundamental analysts, macro investors, and financial advisors who require deep market data, global coverage, and customizable analytics.

Starting at free and climbing to $119/month for the Premier tier, Koyfin's pricing structure is straightforward with no hidden subscription traps. The platform covers stocks, ETFs, forex, crypto, and fixed-income instruments across global markets, making it versatile for investors tracking everything from US equities to international macro indicators.

All Koyfin Plans Compared

Koyfin offers four distinct pricing tiers, each designed for different user needs. Here's the complete breakdown:

Plan Monthly Cost Best For
Free $0/mo Casual investors, students, data explorers
Plus $25/mo Active retail investors, small portfolios
Pro $55/mo Serious traders, fundamental analysts
Premier $119/mo Financial advisors, institutional users

Free Tier ($0/month)

Koyfin's free plan is legitimately generous for a financial data platform. You get access to fundamental data, macro indicators, customizable watchlists, and basic charting tools. The free tier includes:

  • Real-time and historical price data across major asset classes
  • Fundamental company data (financials, earnings, valuations)
  • Macroeconomic indicators and economic calendars
  • Customizable dashboards and watchlists
  • News feed integration
  • Limited technical analysis tools
  • Mobile app access with basic functionality

The limitation? You'll hit caps on saved watchlists, dashboard customization depth, and advanced analytical features. The free tier is excellent for getting comfortable with Koyfin's interface before committing financially, but power users will quickly outgrow it.

Plus Tier ($25/month)

At $25/month, the Plus tier removes most free restrictions and adds intermediate features for active investors:

  • Everything in the Free tier
  • Expanded watchlist and dashboard capacity
  • Advanced portfolio analytics and performance tracking
  • Enhanced mobile app features
  • Additional custom alerts and notifications
  • API access for basic integrations

This is the sweet spot for retail investors who actively trade or manage diversified portfolios but don't need institutional-level tools. At roughly the cost of a subscription service, Plus delivers genuine utility for individuals tracking multiple positions.

Pro Tier ($55/month)

The Pro tier doubles down on analytical power for serious traders and financial analysts:

  • Everything in the Plus tier
  • Institutional-grade charting tools
  • Advanced screeners and custom filters
  • Deeper macroeconomic data sets
  • Priority customer support
  • Enhanced API access
  • Real-time alerts with custom parameters
  • Fixed-income research and analytics

Pro users gain access to Koyfin's most powerful research tools, making it suitable for professional fundamental analysts, macro investors, and financial advisors managing client portfolios. The charting tools are substantially more capable than the lower tiers, though users familiar with dedicated platforms like TradingView may still notice some limitations in advanced technical analysis options.

Premier Tier ($119/month)

Premier is Koyfin's top tier, targeting institutional clients and professional teams:

  • Everything in the Pro tier
  • Team and multi-user accounts with permissions management
  • Advanced API access for enterprise integrations
  • Dedicated support and onboarding
  • Custom data feeds and integrations
  • Extended data retention and historical archives
  • White-label options for financial advisors

At $119/month, Premier is still a fraction of Bloomberg Terminal's annual cost while offering comparable depth in fundamental and macro data. This tier is designed for wealth management firms, institutional traders, and financial advisors who need collaborative tools and deep data integration.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Koyfin offers a fully functional free tier rather than a limited time trial. There is no expiration date on the free plan, making it an excellent option for investors who want to evaluate the platform long-term without commitment.

The free plan is genuinely useful for:

  • Learning the platform: Explore Koyfin's interface, data quality, and unique features with zero financial commitment.
  • Portfolio tracking: Monitor a handful of positions and receive basic alerts.
  • Market research: Access fundamental data and macro indicators for research and due diligence.
  • Students and educators: Study financial data without subscription costs.

Is the free tier worth it? Absolutely. Even if you never upgrade, Koyfin's free plan provides more utility than many paid financial news websites. The primary limitations are fewer saved views, limited customization, and reduced technical analysis depth—not restricted data access.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Koyfin's pricing is transparent: the monthly subscription is the primary cost. However, a few details worth noting:

  • No exchange fees: Unlike brokerages that charge per trade, Koyfin's subscription covers all data access. There are no transaction costs or execution fees because Koyfin doesn't process trades.
  • No data overages: Unlike some platforms with per-API-call pricing, Koyfin's subscription includes unlimited API requests (at appropriate usage levels).
  • Mobile app: The mobile app is included with any paid tier. It's fully featured on Pro and Premier but more limited on free and Plus plans.
  • No broker integration fees: While Koyfin doesn't integrate with brokers for execution, there are no commission costs for viewing broker data.
  • Annual billing: The platform may offer discounts for annual prepayment, though exact discounts aren't specified in standard pricing. It's worth asking directly.

Unlike Bloomberg Terminal—which charges thousands annually plus exchange fees for premium data feeds—Koyfin's all-inclusive monthly model is genuinely straightforward.

Koyfin Pricing vs Competitors

How does Koyfin's pricing stack against other financial data platforms? Here's a realistic comparison:

Platform Entry Paid Tier Mid-Range Tier Best For
Koyfin $25/mo (Plus) $55/mo (Pro) Fundamental analysts, macro investors
TradingView ~$15/mo (Plus) ~$30/mo (Pro) Technical analysis, charting depth
Interactive Brokers Free (with $0 minimum) Free or pay-per-trade Active traders, execution-focused
Bloomberg Terminal ~$24,000/year (enterprise) $24,000+/year Professional institutions only

Key takeaways from this comparison:

  • vs TradingView: TradingView is cheaper for basic charting ($15 vs $25), but Koyfin includes deeper fundamental data. TradingView's Pro tier ($30) offers superior technical charting, while Koyfin's Pro tier ($55) adds institutional data depth.
  • vs Interactive Brokers: Interactive Brokers offers free platforms with broker integration and execution capability, but lacks Koyfin's fundamental research depth and dedicated data terminal experience.
  • vs Bloomberg Terminal: Koyfin is 240x cheaper annually ($1,428 for Premier vs $24,000+), making it genuinely accessible for retail professionals who need Bloomberg-quality data without Bloomberg's enterprise pricing.

Is Koyfin Worth the Price?

The honest answer: it depends on your investing style and how much you value quality data.

Best Value for Each Tier:

  • Free ($0): Absolutely worth it if you want to test Koyfin or casually track markets. No commitment, genuine functionality.
  • Plus ($25/mo): Worth it if you actively manage a personal portfolio and want professional-grade fundamental data without breaking the bank. Retail investors tracking 5-50 positions will benefit immediately.
  • Pro ($55/mo): Justified for serious traders, financial analysts, and advisors who rely on deep research. The advanced screening and macro data justify the premium over Plus.
  • Premier ($119/mo): Worth it only if you're running a firm, managing multiple client portfolios, or need team collaboration. For individual investors, this tier is oversized—Pro is typically sufficient.

When NOT to upgrade:

  • If you primarily execute trades and don't research fundamentals, TradingView or a broker platform is better suited.
  • If you don't use macro data or international markets, the free tier may suffice indefinitely.
  • If you need broker integration or automated trading, Koyfin isn't designed for that—you'll need a brokerage platform.

Koyfin's unique value proposition:

What justifies Koyfin's pricing is the institutional-quality fundamental data and macro analytics that typically cost thousands annually. If you spend time on fundamental research, comparing valuations across markets, or tracking economic indicators, Koyfin's data depth provides real competitive advantage at a fraction of Bloomberg's cost.

How to Save on Koyfin

While Koyfin's pricing is already competitive, here are ways to optimize your costs:

  • Start free: The free tier is feature-rich. If you can operate within its constraints, you save $25-119/month indefinitely.
  • Annual billing: Contact Koyfin directly about annual prepayment discounts. Many SaaS platforms offer 15-20% discounts for annual commitments.
  • Downgrade strategically: If you use Koyfin heavily for 3-6 months then casually, downgrade to Plus or free during slow periods. No lock-in contracts required.
  • Team accounts: If multiple people need access, Premier's team capabilities may cost less per-person than individual subscriptions.
  • Student/educator pricing: Check if Koyfin offers educational discounts—many fintech platforms do.
  • Compare total value: If you're currently paying for Bloomberg Terminal, financial data subscriptions, and charting platforms separately, consolidating to Koyfin Plus or Pro could save hundreds monthly.

Bottom line on Koyfin's pricing: It's designed to be transparent and accessible. The free tier is genuinely valuable, Plus is an excellent entry point for active investors, and Pro delivers institutional research capabilities at retail prices. Premium features don't artificially inflate the cost—you're paying for real depth in fundamental and macro data that Bloomberg charges 10-16x more to access.

For more details, check the full Koyfin review on TradingToolsHub, or explore how Koyfin compares to other platforms in your workflow.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
